original: New volcano Paracutin erupts in farmer's corn field (Mexico)
Scientists successfully regenerate the flowering plant Silene stenophylla from a 31,800-year-old piece of fruit, greatly surpassing the previous record of 2,000 years
First description of a dinosaur published - ‘Megalosaurus or great Fossil Lizard of Stonesfield’ to the Geological Society of London by William Buckland - the beginning of dinosaur mania
John Glennbecomes the first American to orbit Earth aboard Friendship 7
Kepler-37b, the smallest known exoplanet, is discovered
Programming language Python is released by Guido van Rossum
Batman & Robin comic strip premieres in newspapers
Jimi Hendrix(16), rock and roll guitarist, plays his first gig in the Temple De Hirsch synagogue basement, Seattle; fired from the band after the 1st set due to "wild" playing
Elizabeth Holloway Marston, American psychologist co-designed predecessor to the polygraph, and an inspiration for Wonder Woman, born on the Isle of Man (d. 1993)
Dane Caroline Mikkelson is 1st woman to land on Antarctica
Tropical Cyclone Winston hits Fiji, killing at least 29 - most powerful storm on record in Southern Hemisphere winds 296 kilometers per hour (184 mph)
Estonia becomes the first country to establish a national system of fast chargers for electric cars
NASA's Ranger 8 probe completes planned "impact trajectory" on the Moon,  successfully transmitting myriad photos and other data
Chinese space agency, The China Academy of Space Technology is established in Beijing
Raven Wilkinson, American ballerina, first African American woman to dance with major ballet company, born in New York City (d. 2018)
Lt E H O'Hare single-handedly shoots down 5 Japanese heavy bombers, becomes America's first World War II flying ace
National Emergency Center erroneously orders US radio & TV stations to go off the air. Mistake wasn't resolved for 30 minutes
A gigantic statue of Albania's long-time dictator,Enver Hoxha, is brought down in the Albanian capital, Tirana, by mobs of angry protesters
After defending his WBC flyweight championship, Sot Chitalada's check for $104,000 is stolen by a ringside pickpocket
Panama-Pacific International Exposition (World's Fair) opens in San Francisco
Brian Boitanowins Olympic gold medal in figure skating
After winning the Olympic ice dance gold medal in Vancouver (2010), Canadian pair Tessa Virtue & Scott Moir follow their team event gold by taking the ice dance title at the Pyeongchang Winter Games
Andre Hoffmann skates world record 1500m (1:52.06)
Marie-Philip Poulin scores in overtime to lead Canada to a 3-2 win over the US and 4th consecutive Olympic women's ice hockey gold medal at the Sochi Winter Games
Quebec's Czech rookie brothers Peter and Anton Šťastný each score first career NHL hat-tricks in Nordiques' 9-3 win at Vancouver; Peter adds 3 assists and Anton 2
After winning the individual large hill 10k Nordic combined title Jørgen Graabak of Norway wins his 2nd gold medal of the Sochi Winter Olympics in the team large hill / 4 x 5 km relay
Soviet cross-country skierNikolay Zimyatovwins his 2nd gold medal of the Lake Placid Winter Olympics as part of the 4 x 10k relay; also wins 30k and 50k gold
"Mr. Hockey,"Gordie Howescores a goal and adds 3 assists, including his 1,800th career NHL point, in 6-5 Red Wings' win over Buffalo in Detroit; Howe's 1,670th game
Future Baseball Hall of Fame outfielderWillie Maysbecomes the highest-paid player in MLB, signing a record $100,000 contract with the San Francisco Giants
Leonard Baichan scores 105* on Test Cricket debut, v Pakistan Lahore
Australian cricket greats Neil Harvey and Alan Davidson play final Test match in drawn 5th Test vs England at the Sydney Cricket Ground
Emmett Ashford is certified to be first black umpire in organised baseball; has to wait until 1966 for MLB debut
The Beatles record "That Means A Lot"; unhappy with the results, it was given to P.J. Proby to record; the original Beatles version was unreleased until 1996 on "Anthology 2"
Benjamin Waugh, English clergyman and social reformer who founded the National Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children, born in Settle, North Yorkshire, England (d. 1908)
Joshua Slocum, Canadian-American seaman and adventurer (first man to sail around the world alone), born in Mount Hanley, Nova Scotia (d. 1909)
Paul Tripp, American TV host (Mr I Magination), songwriter (Tubby the Tuba), and children's book writer (Rabbi Santa Claus), born in New York City (d. 2002)
Buffy Sainte-Marie, American folk singer (Now That the Buffalo Are Gone), and songwriter ("Up Where We Belong"), born in Stoneham, Massachusetts
Dame Gillian Lynne [Pyrke], British dancer, choreographer and actress (Cats; The Phantom of the Opera), born in Bromley, Kent (d. 2018)
Andrew Fabian, British X-ray astronomer known for his work on accreting black holes, born in Surrey, England
Robin Weiss, British molecular biologist and virologist best known for his work on HIV, born in London
Henry Eyring, Mexican-American chemist (chemical reaction rates), born in Colonia Juárez, Mexico (d. 1981)
Fred Robinson, American jazz trombonist (Louis Armstong's Hot Five; Fats Waller; Cab Calloway), born in Memphis, Tennessee (d. 1984)
Rosalind Laura Burke, British aviator and aircraft factory manager during WWII, born in England (d. 1994)
Nancy Wilson, American Grammy Award-winning R&B and jazz vocalist ("Guess Who I Saw Today"; "(You Don't Know) How Glad I Am"), and actress, born in Chillicothe, Ohio (d. 2018)
Richard Matheson, American author known for "I Am Legend", born in Allendale, New Jersey (d. 2013)
Ludwig Boltzmann, Austrian physicist (thermodynamics), born in Vienna, Austria (d. 1906)
Clyde Wright, American MLB pitcher (no-hitter v Oakland 1970), born in Jefferson City, Tennessee
Aleksandr Pavlovich Alexandrov, Russian cosmonaut (Soyuz T-9, TM-3), born in Moscow, Soviet Union
Billy Zoom [Stuart Kindell], American punk rock guitarist (X), born in Savanna, Illinois
Anthony Davis, American pianist, Pulitzer Prize winning composer (The Central Park Five; X), and professor of music (University of California), born in Paterson, New Jersey
Chinese diplomat Zhou Daguan departs Mingzhou for Angkor, chief city of the Khmer Empire; he will be the first foreigner to describe life in the city
The steam packet-ship John Rutledge, en route from Liverpool to New York, hits an iceberg and sinks with the loss of 120 passengers and 19 crew; only one survivor (Thomas Nye of New Bedford)
English Captain James Weddell reaches 74°15' S, 1520 km from South Pole
Massachusetts Institute of Technology forms 1st US collegiate architectural school
New York's Metropolitan Museum of Art opens
Roland Liboton becomes world champ cross-country cycling
Japan launches Tenma satellite to study x-rays (450/570 km)
19th Daytona 500:Cale Yarboroughwins his 2nd Great American Race; Janet Guthrie first female NASCAR Cup Series driver; finishes 12th
Flight readiness firing of Columbia's main engines; 20 seconds
KingEdward VIof England crowned following the death of his father Henry VIII
